title: hibbertia fractiflexa subsp. fractiflexa
intro: Hibbertia fractiflexa subsp. fractiflexa, also known as the Fractified Guinea Flower, is a rare and unique plant species native to Western Australia. It is a member of the Dilleniaceae family and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hibbertia fractiflexa subsp. fractiflexa include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. This plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall skin health.
